title: Onboard a 2nd framework through the framework-terms gate
context: OER are abundant and free but are not indexed against the curriculum standards that govern what teachers must teach, so teachers cannot find the free resource aligned to a given standard. This project builds an open crosswalk of educator-reviewed AlignmentRecords linking OER to standards by stable identifier (CASE GUID / ASN URI), not by reproducing copyrighted standards text. Two hard gates never skip: the dual licence/terms gate (gate-002) and educator review of every record, with a blind second-reviewer precision audit holding precision at >= 95%. Reaching >= 2 frameworks requires running a second framework through the framework-terms gate.
objective: Onboard a second standards framework through the framework-terms gate and add it to the registry.
acceptanceCriteria: A second framework is run through gate-002 framework-terms: permitsReference and permitsTextReproduction recorded from cited terms with a snapshot.
The framework's machine-readable source (CASE registry / official open data) is identified and its identifiers (CASE GUID / ASN URI) resolve.
Added to the frameworks registry (registry-007); permitsReference:false implies excluded.
Output licensed CC-BY-4.0; the commit is DCO signed-off.
